title: Tragacantha cruckshanksii
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Tragacantha cruckshanksii". However, I found information on the plant "Tragacanth" (Astragalus gummifer), which is a type of shrub that belongs to the legume family. 

Here's a paragraph about Tragacanth (Astragalus gummifer):

Tragacanth, specifically Tragacanth gum, is a natural polysaccharide derived from the sap of the Astragalus gummifer shrub. The health benefits of Tragacanth include soothing digestive issues, such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include polysaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include capsules, powders, and suspensions, often used in traditional medicine to treat gastrointestinal issues and as a natural thickening agent.
taxonomy: Tragacantha cruckshanksii is classified under the family Fabaceae and the genus Astragalus is not correct. Tragacantha cruckshanksii is a synonym for the species which is also classified as Astragalus cruckshanksii. Further classification under the family Fabaceae is accurate as this family contains a wide range of legume species. Astragalus cruckshanksii is a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand. The classification under Fabaceae and Astragalus cruckshanksii accurately reflects its botanical identity.

properties: Tragacantha cruckshanksii, also known as New Zealand spiny saltbush, has been traditionally used for its potential therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage.
